title: SADF explosiveness gauge on log-close (structural-break feature, AFML Ch17.4.2)
source: LdP literature mining (2 parallel read-only agents over AFML Ch5/17/18/19 + HFT, books in D:/PROJECTS/QuantTradingDocs/extracted), 2026-06-03. Conversation: 'hay alguna otra nueva feature que se puede extraer de la literatura'.
why: The current set captures momentum/vol LEVEL but nothing about whether the price PATH is in a unit-root vs explosive (bubble) regime. SADF = sup of backward-expanding ADF t-stats (AFML §17.4.2) spikes during bubble-like behavior and is maximally orthogonal to rv_regime (realized-vol percentile) and z_r20/macd (point-in-time momentum). NOTE: a plain CUSUM event-filter already exists (cusum_filter primary) — this is a different object (an explosiveness FEATURE), not that.
value_rationale: WORTH-TESTING. Maximally orthogonal structural-break dimension, but the one HEAVY-compute candidate (O(T^2) ADF fits) — must cap the backward window.
scope: Implement SADF on log(close), right-fixed/backward-expanding window per §17.4.2; H0: beta<=0 vs H1: beta>0
CAP the backward window (e.g. 252-500 bars) so compute stays in minutes on ~5000 bars; re-check cost before H4 (more bars)
Causality: each SADF_t uses only bars <= t; any QADF/CADF quantile normalization must be within-window, NOT full-sample; min-sample tau a fixed constant
Consider QADF (0.95 quantile) over raw SADF for robustness to sampling frequency
recommendation: Test with a windowed (capped) implementation; log prices not raw. Lowest priority of the five due to compute.
out_of_scope: Full expanding-to-bar-1 SADF (O(T^2) blowup). Chow single-break / BDE-CSW CUSUM tests (SADF supersedes for a rolling feature).

history.reason: LdP literature mining: SADF explosiveness gauge = orthogonal structural-break feature (heavy compute, cap window)
verification: SADF confirmed; fold in a cheap CUSUM (Chu-Stinchcombe-White) companion + signed sub/super-martingale (SMT) variant with phi-penalty
